Observe.Resolve.Automate.
FunctionsArchitectureUse CasesLawsToolsTrainingAbout
LawsLaw II: Demand Inelasticity
IILaw II

Demand Inelasticity

Subscribers do not reduce consumption when quality degrades. They churn. Demand is inelastic to price within the loyalty window.

The intervention window — the period between first degradation and churn decision — is the operator's only actionable asset.

The Mechanism

Unlike most markets, telecom subscribers cannot easily reduce their network usage when quality drops. They need connectivity. When quality falls below tolerance, they do not consume less — they leave. The loyalty window varies by segment: enterprise contracts extend it, consumer prepaid compresses it to days. Whoever can identify the high-ARPU subscriber in that window can intervene.

At L4 — Autonomous Networks

At L4, the network identifies individual subscriber degradation in real time and triggers personalised intervention — proactive compensation, priority routing, or case management — before the subscriber is aware a problem occurred.

Derived Functions

F2mandatory

Subscriber Experience Segmentation

Which specific subscribers are experiencing degradation right now, and what is their ARPU value?

Law IILaw VI
◑●○◑●
F4conditional

Competitive Position Measurement

How does the delivered network experience compare to what competitors deliver in the same geography?

Law V
○○●●○

Anchored Use Cases

Law IIF2combination

High-ARPU Pre-Churn Detection

Passive probe identifies subscriber with ARPU >€50 experiencing sustained MOS below 3.5 for 48 hours. No complaint raised.

L1L2L3L4
High-ARPU subscriber retained before entering the churn decision window.
